Finance & Economy.headline: Inflation shoots up to 9.13% in February, highest in 10 months
Finance & Economy.detail: National general inflation in Bangladesh rose to 9.13% in February 2026, which is the highest in 10 months, driven by a spike in food inflation. Despite the monthly rise, the current figures remain slightly lower than those recorded in the same period last year.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK must be prepared for a price shock from the Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: Heather Stewart reports that the UK needs to prepare for potential price increases as a result of the ongoing war with Iran. The conflict could disrupt global markets and lead to economic instability.

Finance & Economy.headline: White House worries as gas prices jump amid ongoing US-Israel war on Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: The US-Israel war on Iran has caused a jump in gas prices, creating concerns for the White House. Rising tensions are rippling through global oil markets, sending crude prices sharply higher.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gas prices surge as Iran conflict rattles global oil markets, pushing US crude above $90
Finance & Economy.detail: Rising tensions with Iran are having an impact on global oil markets, leading to a sharp increase in crude prices and pushing U.S. gas prices higher. The conflict's effect on energy markets is causing concerns about potential economic repercussions.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks fell, GIFT Nifty down nearly 300 points and oil nears $100. How will stock market react on Monday?
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks experienced a decline, and oil prices are nearing $100, raising questions about the stock market's reaction on Monday. Investors are closely watching market trends.

Technology.headline: 'HK, GBA tech parks can yield cutting-edge advances'
Technology.detail: Businessman Irons Sze has called for collaboration between technology parks in Hong Kong and the Greater Bay Area to make advances in artificial intelligence and biotechnology, focusing on cutting-edge technology and establishing more national-level scientific research platforms.

Technology.headline: Oracle Layoffs: Tech giant to slash 30,000 jobs as banks pull out from financing AI data centres
Technology.detail: Oracle is reportedly planning to cut 30,000 jobs following banks pulling out from financing AI data centers.

Technology.headline: The rise and fall of a 3D printing empire
Technology.detail: An industrial 3D printer, the P-50, by Desktop Metal that sounded like magic never worked well enough to sell and was quietly shelved, an early warning of how much the 3D printing industry's rapid growth relied on promises that have not come to fruition.

Technology.headline: Humans are being replaced by machines in the food supply chain — and it's leading to truckloads of waste
Technology.detail: A researcher explores how AI is being used to optimize food delivery, which may not always be a good thing, leading to humans being replaced by machines in the food supply chain.

Technology.headline: ACM Technology Policy Council Warns Against 'False Choice' AI Strategies
Technology.detail: A new TechBrief from ACM's Technology Policy Council outlines a strategic framework for governments evaluating how to acquire and deploy national-scale AI systems, warning that improperly structured decisions could expose countries to vendor lock-in, capability gaps, climbing costs, and loss of digital sovereignty.

Science.headline: A new “magic mushroom” drug could treat depression without psychedelic hallucinations
Science.detail: Scientists are exploring a new way to harness the medical promise of psychedelic compounds without the mind-bending side effects by creating modified versions of psilocin that target serotonin pathways linked to depression but cause fewer psychedelic-like effects.

Science.headline: A perfectly balanced atom just broke one of nuclear physics' biggest rules
Science.detail: Physicists have discovered a hidden “Island of Inversion” in molybdenum-84, revealing that even perfectly balanced nuclei can suddenly warp into exotic shapes. For decades, such regions were thought to exist only in neutron-rich isotopes.

Science.headline: China could see widespread use of brain-computer tech in 3-5 years, expert says
Science.detail: A leading BCI expert says that China could see brain-computer interface (BCI) technology move into practical public use within three to five years as products mature, as Beijing races to catch up with U.S. startups including Elon Musk's Neuralink. New policies and integration into national medical insurance aim to close the gap between scientific research, industry, and clinical applications.

Science.headline: SpaceX wants to launch a million satellites. Here's how that could impact the atmosphere and the night sky
Science.detail: Scientists are deeply concerned about SpaceX's recent proposal to launch one million satellites into orbit around Earth. Their concerns range from a loss of the natural night sky and our access to space, to the environmental impact on our atmosphere.

Science.headline: A new study finds dangerous chemicals in some commonly used hair extensions
Science.detail: A new study has found dangerous chemicals in some commonly used hair extensions. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to researcher Elissia Franklin about the findings.

Health.headline: Mayo Clinic Discovers Rare Gene Mutation That Causes Fatty Liver Disease
Health.detail: Mayo Clinic researchers have identified a rare mutation in the MET gene that can directly cause metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover Protein That Triggers Diabetic Blindness
Health.detail: A newly identified protein may hold the key to preventing diabetic blindness, as researchers discovered that LRG1 triggers the earliest damage in diabetic retinopathy by constricting tiny retinal blood vessels and reducing oxygen supply.

Health.headline: Cannabis Compounds CBD and CBG May Help Reverse Fatty Liver Disease, Study Finds
Health.detail: Researchers have found that cannabis compounds CBD and CBG may help reverse fatty liver disease.

Health.headline: Eating Less Protein May Slow Liver Cancer Growth, Study Finds
Health.detail: A Rutgers-led study found that eating less protein may help slow liver cancer in people with impaired liver function because when damaged livers can't properly clear toxic ammonia from protein metabolism, the excess ammonia can feed tumor growth.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover the Switch That Revives Exhausted Cancer-fighting T Cells
Health.detail: Researchers have discovered a new way to increase a key brain protein damaged in Rett syndrome, a rare genetic disorder that affects thousands of children worldwide, and early studies in mice and patient-derived cells show the approach can restore function.

Sports.headline: India takes on New Zealand in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final tonight
Sports.detail: The final match will be played tonight.

Sports.headline: Hamilton left furious as Russell capitalises for Australian GP victory
Sports.detail: George Russell won the Australian Grand Prix, with Hamilton expressing his anger.

Sports.headline: All England Final Live: Lakshya Sen Fights Back, Turns The Tide In Game 1
Sports.detail: Lakshya Sen is competing in the All England Final, with live updates available.

Sports.headline: WVU women advance to Big 12 basketball title game
Sports.detail: West Virginia's women's basketball team advanced to the Big 12 tournament championship game after a late-game steal.

Sports.headline: IWF celebrates International Women's Day
Sports.detail: The International Weightlifting Federation (IWF) celebrates women in the sport and their growing participation.

Entertainment.headline: Finland President Alexander Stubb watched Dhurandhar, says 'I look forward to the sequel'
Entertainment.detail: Finland President Alexander Stubb, during his state visit to India, revealed he watched Ranveer Singh's spy thriller Dhurandhar and expressed excitement for its sequel, Dhurandhar: The Revenge, which is set to release on March 19, 2026. The makers dropped the trailer of Dhurandhar: The Revenge on March 7, which received praise from celebrities including Alia Bhatt, Anil Kapoor and Vicky Kaushal, among others.

Entertainment.headline: Masataka Yoshida lifts quarterfinal-bound Japan to victory with late two-run homer
Entertainment.detail: Masataka Yoshida's two-run homer in the seventh inning led Japan to a 4-3 victory over Australia in the 2026 World Baseball Classic. Japan had already qualified for the quarterfinal round of the 2026 World Baseball Classic before taking the field against Australia on Sunday night.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears Fired Her Sobriety Coaches Before Arrest
Entertainment.detail: Britney Spears fired her sobriety coaches before being arrested on suspicion of DUI in California. She is out of police custody after the arrest.

Entertainment.headline: Liberty Media Corporation Announces 2026 Virtual Annual Meeting of Stockholders
Entertainment.detail: Liberty Media Corporation announced its 2026 Virtual Annual Meeting of Stockholders. The meeting details are yet to be released.

Entertainment.headline: Review: 'The NewZealand Story: Untold Adventure' is a welcome shot of nostalgia
Entertainment.detail: Bitobit has released 'The NewZealand Story: Untold Adventure', officially licensed by TAITO, on Steam. The game retains the familiar level designs where Tiki the Kiwi navigates through enemies to free a trapped Kiwi at the end of each level, with a cleaner and more vibrant look for Steam gamers.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ian Huntley, one of UK's most notorious child killers, has died, days after being attacked in prison
Crime & Justice.detail: One of Britain's most notorious child killers has died, days after he was attacked by a fellow inmate in the workshop of a maximum security prison.

Crime & Justice.headline: Judge voids Kari Lake's actions as acting CEO of U.S. media agency
Crime & Justice.detail: A federal judge ruled Saturday that Kari Lake did not have legal authority to take the actions she's done to largely dismantle the Voice of America.

Crime & Justice.headline: 4 men arrested in UK on suspicion of aiding Iran by spying on Jewish community
Crime & Justice.detail: Four men have been arrested in the UK on suspicion of aiding Iran by spying on the Jewish community.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rapist who targeted woman after she left nightclub is sentenced after he blamed family member for the attack
Crime & Justice.detail: A man who raped a woman after she left a Southampton nightclub has today been sentenced for his horrific crimes – after trying to blame his actions on a family member.

Crime & Justice.headline: Georgia sheriff arrested on DUI charge after blood alcohol content was allegedly almost triple legal limit
Crime & Justice.detail: A Georgia sheriff was arrested for allegedly driving a county vehicle with a blood alcohol level nearly three times the legal limit late last month while driving a county vehicle.

Education.headline: JEE Advanced 2026: Exam on May 17; application starts on April 23
Education.detail: The Joint Entrance Examination (JEE) Advanced 2026 exam is scheduled for May 17, with the application process commencing on April 23.

Education.headline: How China's education reforms are shaping a brighter future
Education.detail: China's education system is undergoing profound transformation with a focus on equitable access, high-quality standards and strategic importance for national rejuvenation, highlighted by Minister of Education Huai Jinpeng. The Chinese education system includes 440,000 schools, 280 million students, and 18.7 million teachers.

Education.headline: Medical College assessment reports matter raised in Parliament, Centre cites NMC website disclosures
Education.detail: The Union Health Minister Anupriya Patel informed the Lok Sabha that information on medical colleges, course recognition, seat availability, regulations and admissions are displayed on the National Medical Commission (NMC) website. The Minister addressed concerns about the NMC withholding Annual Medical College Assessment Reports despite orders to upload them annually.

Education.headline: Today News Headlines for School Assembly, March 9, 2026: T20 World Cup 2026
Education.detail: News headlines for school assembly on March 9, 2026 include coverage of the T20 World Cup 2026 final between India and New Zealand.

Education.headline: International Women's Day: As AI reshapes work, Indian women are adapting faster
Education.detail: Indian women are adapting faster as AI reshapes work.

Lifestyle.headline: Heart disease threat projected to climb sharply for key demographic
Lifestyle.detail: A new American Heart Association report predicts 59% of women will have high blood pressure by 2050, up from 49% today, in addition to higher diabetes and obesity rates.

Lifestyle.headline: Passengers rip airline for new seating policy: 'It is as bad as everyone is saying'
Lifestyle.detail: Southwest Airlines faces passenger backlash over a new assigned seating policy, with flyers complaining about higher prices and boarding issues.

Lifestyle.headline: Allu Arjun and Sneha Reddy look stunning as they celebrate 15th anniversary
Lifestyle.detail: Lifestyle news reports on Allu Arjun and Sneha Reddy's 15th-anniversary celebration.

Lifestyle.headline: Therapist shares what she asked an AI chatbot girlfriend during couple's therapy
Lifestyle.detail: A therapist incorporated an AI chatbot girlfriend into a couple's therapy session, exploring the evolving dynamics of relationships in the age of AI.

Lifestyle.headline: Holi in the deep: Milind Soman and Ankita Konwar swap gulaal for a 15km Goa swim
Lifestyle.detail: Milind Soman and Ankita Konwar celebrated Holi by swimming 15km in Goa, foregoing the traditional gulaal.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Can the military and AI firms get along?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The whole country suffers when government needlessly punishes innovative firms, hindering collaboration between the military and AI companies. This editorial discusses the importance of fostering a better relationship between the military and AI firms for the benefit of the nation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The war that would make Iran look mild
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Delaying arms shipments to Taiwan undermines deterrence at a dangerous time, increasing the risk of a larger conflict. The editorial board argues that weakening Taiwan's defenses is a dangerous strategy.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Imperial war: on the war against Iran
Opinion/Editorial.detail: India should oppose the U.S.'s attempt to widen the war to the Indian Ocean. The article discusses the Israeli-American war against Iran and its implications.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: As Iran confronts existential threat, its minorities will shape internal contest and political modernisation in Middle East.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: C. Raja Mohan writes about how Iran's minorities will influence the internal conflicts and political modernization in the Middle East as Iran faces an existential threat.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on AI in war: the Iran conflict shows that the paradigm shift has already begun.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the implications of AI in warfare, particularly in the context of the Iran conflict, highlighting a significant shift in military strategy.

World.headline: Iran oil facilities hit for first time as war with US-Israel enters day 9
World.detail: The US and Israel continue to strike Iran, hitting oil storage depots and refining facilities for the first time, escalating the ongoing conflict. Plumes of smoke were seen rising over Tehran after the attacks.

World.headline: Middle East crisis live: Israel warns it will pursue Iran's next supreme leader; smoke fills Tehran after missiles strike oil depots
World.detail: Israel has warned that it will target the next Supreme Leader of Iran and is continuing strikes on Iranian oil depots, resulting in heavy smoke in Tehran. The Israeli military stated they would target anyone seeking to appoint a successor to the killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

World.headline: Tehran sky turns black as oil sites hit; Bahrain desalination plant struck
World.detail: Iranian attacks have damaged a water desalination plant in Bahrain, and oil sites in Iran have been struck, causing the sky in Tehran to darken. These events are part of the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran.

World.headline: Gas Prices Surge in U.S. as Iran War Chokes Oil Supply
World.detail: The price of gasoline in the U.S. has increased by 14% in one week and is expected to rise further due to the disruption of oil supplies caused by the war in Iran. This surge in gas prices is impacting consumers across the country.

World.headline: Iran has chosen new supreme leader after killing of Ayatollah Khamenei in US-Israel strike - state media
World.detail: Following the death of Ayatollah Khamenei in a US-Israel strike, Iran has selected a new Supreme Leader, according to state media. This development comes amidst heightened tensions and ongoing conflict in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: Woman strangles to death newly-born baby, buries body in shallow grave
Zimbabwe.detail: A woman has been arrested after strangling her newly-born baby to death and burying the body in a shallow grave. The incident has shocked the local community.

Zimbabwe.headline: Dynamos and Tel One match abandoned due to waterlogged pitch
Zimbabwe.detail: The Dynamos and Tel One match was abandoned due to a waterlogged pitch. Heavy rains rendered the field unplayable, leading to the game's cancellation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Don't weaponise social media to settle personal scores— court tells man for cyberbullying ex-girlfriend
Zimbabwe.detail: A court has cautioned against using social media to settle personal disputes after a man was found guilty of cyberbullying his ex-girlfriend. The court emphasized the importance of responsible social media usage.

Zimbabwe.headline: AMH boss admits salary failures
Zimbabwe.detail: The Africa Media Holdings (AMH) boss has acknowledged shortcomings regarding salary payments. This admission comes amidst concerns about employee compensation within the organization.

Zimbabwe.headline: Defiant Madhuku vows to stop Mnangagwa's term extension despite crackdown on anti-2030 proponents.
Zimbabwe.detail: Despite the crackdown on those opposing the extension of President Mnangagwa's term, Madhuku remains steadfast in his commitment to prevent it. He is vowing to fight against the proposed extension to 2030.
